learningOutcomes: Identify different forms of business organisations
Compare sole proprietorship, partnership, and company
Explain the concept of limited liability
realLifeContexts: Why a small tea stall is a sole proprietorship while Tata Group is a company
How limited liability protects shareholders from personal loss
Why cooperatives like Amul are different from private companies
commonMisconceptions: Private limited and public limited companies are the same
All partnerships have unlimited liability
Cooperative societies aim to maximize profit like companies

learningOutcomes: Explain the formation and winding up of companies
Compare public sector, private sector, and joint sector enterprises
Evaluate advantages and disadvantages of each form
Describe the role of Memorandum and Articles of Association
realLifeContexts: Why ONGC and BHEL are PSUs and what challenges they face
How McDonald's operates in India via a franchise model
Why multinationals face host country regulations
commonMisconceptions: Disinvestment means the government completely exits a company
Joint ventures are only for large multinational corporations
Franchise owners are fully independent entrepreneurs

learningOutcomes: Analyze corporate governance frameworks
Evaluate the role and challenges of PSUs in a liberalized economy
Assess ethical issues in corporate organization (CSR, stakeholder theory)
Compare Indian and global business organization models
realLifeContexts: Why the IL&FS crisis raised corporate governance concerns
How the Companies Act 2013 mandates 2% profit CSR spending
Satyam scandal as a corporate governance failure case study
commonMisconceptions: Corporate governance is only relevant for large listed companies
CSR spending is voluntary and unregulated in India
Independent directors are fully independent in all circumstances

learningOutcomes: Define management and explain its importance
List and describe Henri Fayol's principles of management
Compare Fayol's and Taylor's approaches to management
realLifeContexts: Why a school uses division of work among teachers
How McDonald's uses scientific management principles in kitchen operations
Why unity of command prevents confusion in organizations
commonMisconceptions: Management and administration are exactly the same
Taylor's scientific management treats workers well
Fayol's principles are rigid rules that must be followed exactly

learningOutcomes: Explain the functions of management in detail
Describe levels of management and their roles
Apply management principles to real business scenarios
Explain the importance of coordination as the essence of management
realLifeContexts: How a hospital manages three levels of management for patient care
Why decentralization helped Infosys scale globally
How MBO aligns individual goals with organizational goals
commonMisconceptions: Delegation reduces a manager's authority and responsibility
Coordination is a separate function from other management functions
Top management deals with day-to-day operations

learningOutcomes: Evaluate modern management theories (systems approach, contingency theory)
Analyze leadership styles and their situational effectiveness
Apply OD (Organizational Development) interventions
Assess management challenges in VUCA environments
realLifeContexts: Why Amazon's leadership principles embed management philosophy in culture
How Satya Nadella's transformational leadership revived Microsoft
Why COVID-19 forced organizations to adopt VUCA-compatible management
commonMisconceptions: There is one best management style for all situations
Transformational leadership is always superior to transactional
VUCA only applies to multinational corporations

learningOutcomes: Define marketing and distinguish it from selling
Explain the marketing mix (4Ps)
Describe different types of markets
realLifeContexts: Why Coca-Cola's marketing is different from a local kirana store's selling
How Apple uses all 4Ps to create premium positioning
Why Amul's marketing strategy has made it a household brand
commonMisconceptions: Marketing and selling are the same activity
The product is the only important P in the marketing mix
Marketing only applies to consumer goods companies

learningOutcomes: Explain product life cycle and strategies for each stage
Describe branding, labelling, and packaging decisions
Analyze pricing strategies and their objectives
Explain channels of distribution and promotion mix
realLifeContexts: How Jio used penetration pricing to gain market share rapidly
Why Apple uses price skimming for iPhone launches
How Dettol brand equity commands premium pricing despite competition
commonMisconceptions: A product in decline stage must always be discontinued
Higher price always signals better quality to consumers
Advertising and promotion are synonymous terms

learningOutcomes: Apply STP (Segmentation, Targeting, Positioning) strategy
Evaluate digital marketing and its integration with traditional marketing
Analyze consumer behavior models
Assess ethical and social responsibility dimensions of marketing
realLifeContexts: How Zomato uses digital marketing and STP for hyperlocal targeting
Why Dove's 'Real Beauty' campaign is cause-related marketing
How FMCG companies use neuromarketing for package design
commonMisconceptions: Digital marketing has replaced traditional marketing completely
Market segmentation means targeting only one very small group
Green marketing is only for environmental companies

learningOutcomes: Define HRM and explain its importance
Describe the recruitment and selection process
Explain basic concepts of training and development
realLifeContexts: How Infosys recruits thousands of engineers each year
Why TCS invests heavily in training new hires
Why a good job description reduces hiring mismatches
commonMisconceptions: HRM is only about hiring and firing employees
Training and development are the same activity
Small businesses don't need formal HRM practices

learningOutcomes: Explain performance appraisal methods and their limitations
Describe compensation and benefits management
Analyze employee motivation theories (Maslow, Herzberg)
Explain industrial relations and grievance handling
realLifeContexts: How Google uses OKRs (similar to KRA/KPI) for performance management
Why Herzberg found salary alone doesn't motivate long-term
How trade unions negotiate wage settlements in manufacturing
commonMisconceptions: Higher salary always motivates employees the most
Performance appraisal is only done annually
360-degree feedback is free from biases

learningOutcomes: Evaluate strategic HRM and its alignment with business strategy
Apply talent management frameworks for succession planning
Analyze diversity and inclusion as business imperatives
Assess HR analytics and people data-driven decision making
realLifeContexts: How Tata Group's talent management produces future leaders
Why Infosys and Wipro invest in employer branding to attract top talent
How HR analytics predicts attrition risk before it happens
commonMisconceptions: Strategic HRM is the same as traditional personnel management
Diversity initiatives are only about gender representation
HR analytics can predict human behavior with certainty

learningOutcomes: Explain the objectives of financial management
Distinguish between fixed capital and working capital
Describe sources of short-term and long-term finance
realLifeContexts: Why a factory needs both long-term finance for machinery and short-term for raw materials
How Reliance raises capital through bonds and equity simultaneously
Why startups often rely on retained earnings initially
commonMisconceptions: Profit maximization is always the best financial objective
Working capital is only needed in manufacturing
Debt financing is always riskier than equity financing

learningOutcomes: Explain capital structure decisions and optimal capital mix
Apply financial leverage and its effect on EPS
Describe dividend policy and its theories
Analyze working capital management: cash, debtors, inventory
realLifeContexts: How Tata Motors' high debt increased financial risk during the 2008 recession
Why IT companies like TCS pay high dividends (excess cash, low capex)
How e-commerce firms manage negative cash conversion cycles
commonMisconceptions: Higher financial leverage always leads to higher profitability
Companies should always pay maximum dividends to shareholders
Working capital management only matters in retail businesses

learningOutcomes: Apply capital budgeting techniques (NPV, IRR, Payback Period)
Evaluate Modigliani-Miller propositions on capital structure
Analyze cost of capital (WACC) and its use in investment decisions
Assess financial risk management using derivatives
realLifeContexts: How Indian airlines hedge fuel price risk using futures contracts
Why WACC is used as the discount rate in DCF valuation
How Infosys uses CAPM to estimate cost of equity for project evaluation
commonMisconceptions: IRR and NPV always give the same investment decision
WACC is always the right discount rate for all projects
Hedging eliminates all financial risk completely

learningOutcomes: Define business environment and its components
Distinguish between micro and macro environment
Explain how environmental changes affect business decisions
realLifeContexts: How demonetization (political change) disrupted businesses overnight
Why e-commerce grew rapidly due to technological environment changes
How changing demographics (social environment) created demand for baby products
commonMisconceptions: Business environment only refers to economic conditions
Micro environment factors are always controllable
Environmental analysis is only done by large corporations

learningOutcomes: Apply SWOT and PESTLE analysis to a business
Explain LPG (Liberalization, Privatization, Globalization) reforms
Analyze the impact of government policies on business
Describe the role of competition in the business environment
realLifeContexts: How 1991 LPG reforms transformed Indian industry
How GST changed the tax environment for businesses
Why FDI in retail created political controversy (PESTLE interaction)
commonMisconceptions: Liberalization means no government role in the economy
SWOT analysis is sufficient without PESTLE analysis
Globalization always benefits developing countries equally

learningOutcomes: Analyze industry dynamics using Porter's Five Forces
Evaluate strategic responses to environmental uncertainty
Assess sustainability and ESG as emerging business environment factors
Analyze digital disruption as a technological environment force
realLifeContexts: How Jio's entry disrupted telecom using Porter's Five Forces perspective
Why Uber's platform model disrupted the taxi industry globally
How ESG factors now affect Nifty 50 company valuations
commonMisconceptions: Porter's Five Forces is static and doesn't account for dynamic markets
ESG reporting is only relevant for listed companies
Digital disruption only affects technology companies

learningOutcomes: Define entrepreneurship and distinguish it from employment
Identify qualities and skills of a successful entrepreneur
Describe the basic steps in starting a business
realLifeContexts: How Flipkart was started by two IIT graduates in a garage
Why Amul's Verghese Kurien is called a social entrepreneur
How an intrepreneur at 3M invented Post-it Notes
commonMisconceptions: Entrepreneurs are only risk-takers not risk managers
Innovation always means inventing something completely new
All startups are entrepreneurial and all entrepreneurs start startups

learningOutcomes: Develop a business plan with key components
Explain funding options for startups (bootstrapping, angel, VC, IPO)
Describe the entrepreneurial process from idea to venture
Analyze the role of government in supporting entrepreneurship (Startup India)
realLifeContexts: How Zepto raised VC funding and became a unicorn in 2 years
Why the Startup India scheme offers tax exemptions for recognized startups
How Boat bootstrapped profitably before raising external funding
commonMisconceptions: All successful startups raise VC funding
A business plan is only needed for bank loans
Unicorn status means the company is profitable

learningOutcomes: Evaluate lean startup methodology and pivoting strategies
Analyze social entrepreneurship and impact measurement
Assess entrepreneurship ecosystem components
Apply design thinking to entrepreneurial problem solving
realLifeContexts: How Instagram pivoted from a location app (Burbn) to a photo app
How Aravind Eye Care is a social entrepreneurship model for healthcare
Why IIT and IIM incubators are key ecosystem components in India
commonMisconceptions: MVP means a low-quality incomplete product
Social entrepreneurs don't need to be financially sustainable
Pivoting always means failing at the original idea

learningOutcomes: Explain the need for consumer protection
List consumer rights under the Consumer Protection Act
Describe how a consumer can file a complaint
realLifeContexts: What a consumer can do if they buy a defective refrigerator
Why food companies must disclose ingredients (right to information)
How online shopping complaints can be filed under the new Consumer Protection Act
commonMisconceptions: Consumer protection laws only apply to expensive products
Only courts can resolve consumer complaints
Consumer rights are the same as consumer responsibilities

learningOutcomes: Distinguish between district, state, and national consumer forums
Explain remedies available to consumers
Describe the role of non-governmental consumer organizations
Analyze the features of the Consumer Protection Act 2019
realLifeContexts: How Amazon India must comply with e-commerce provisions of CPA 2019
Why product liability under CPA 2019 is stricter than earlier law
How NGOs like Consumer Voice educate and represent consumers
commonMisconceptions: National Commission handles only government cases against companies
Mediation under CPA 2019 replaces all court hearings
E-commerce platforms have no liability under consumer protection law

learningOutcomes: Evaluate the effectiveness of consumer protection enforcement in India
Analyze consumer protection in digital markets (data privacy, dark patterns)
Assess international consumer protection frameworks
Apply consumer law principles to complex multi-party transactions
realLifeContexts: How ASCI regulates misleading advertisements in India
Why Amazon and Flipkart face scrutiny for dark patterns in checkout flows
How greenwashing claims lead to consumer complaints against FMCG brands
commonMisconceptions: Data privacy and consumer protection are entirely separate domains
Greenwashing only affects environmental organizations not regular consumers
Consumer redressal in India is as efficient as developed country systems
